title: Bridge Networks, DNS, Ports, Routing, IPv6, and Exposure Policy · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics
summary: A multi-service application works through localhost on one machine but fails by service name and accidentally exposes its database to every interface. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker ipv6 host platform diagnostics increment in least-exposure container network topology. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Diagnose IPv4, IPv6, Desktop VM, host access, DNS, proxy, firewall, MTU, and published-path differences with layered evidence.
Segment frontend, backend, data, egress, internal, and attachable networks and verify which paths remain reachable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics · Theory Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Compose stack publishes its database on every host interface, pins a container IP, and calls localhost from the API. Split trust networks, use service DNS, restrict publishing, and verify ingress separately from egress.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics · Theory Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for diagnose ipv4, ipv6, desktop vm, host access, dns, proxy, firewall, mtu, and published-path differences with layered evidence. For Predict Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics · Theory Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics, keep Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Compose stack publishes its database on every host interface, pins a container IP, and calls localhost from the API. Split trust networks, use service DNS, restrict publishing, and verify ingress separately from egress. Competency probe: Diagnose IPv4, IPv6, Desktop VM, host access, DNS, proxy, firewall, MTU, and published-path differences with layered evidence. A defensible solution applies Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to least-exposure container network topology.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-ipv6-host-platform-diagnostics — Diagnose IPv4, IPv6, Desktop VM, host access, DNS, proxy, firewall, MTU, and published-path differences with layered evidence.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-networking-dns-publishing-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-networking-dns-publishing-4
    artifact: network-contract.yaml
    decision: worked docker networking dns publishing 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      frontend-network: published-edge
      backend-network: internal
      service-discovery: service-name
      host-binding: 127.0.0.1:8080
      database-published: false
    verify: docker network inspect intake-backend --format json
    failure: Reject container IP pinning, localhost-as-peer assumptions, accidental all-interface publishing, or an exposed database port.
    repair: Use project-scoped DNS names, separate networks by trust, bind only the required host interface, and test egress and ingress independently.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.text: One successful ping proves DNS, TCP, TLS, application readiness, and published-port policy. This fails the Docker Ipv6 Host Platform Diagnostics contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
